calendar.matters:
Ex Parte Petition For Order Granting Lien Against Real Property Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-09-2012 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-08-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alex Borden appeared by telephone for Accelerated Inheritance. Mark Malachowski for and with Ana Maria Navarro (Petitioner).

The Court stated that it seemed that the property was put into joint tenancy for convenience and that it was never intended for half of the property to go to Ms. Alvarez. Mr. Malachowski asked that the change in title of the property go back Nunc Pro Tunc to June 13, 1991. Mr. Borden stated that he saw the proposed order and that he wanted to make sure that all of the Family members agreed as to the distribution to Accelerated Inheritance. Mr. Malachowski stated that his Client was prepared to write a check in the amount of $17,000.00 payable to Accelerated Inheritance. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-17-2010 Continued To Jun-29-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Malachowski for and with Anna Navarro. Alex Borden appeared by telephone for Christpoher Malloy.
Mr. Malachowski stated that the cash on hand fluctuated, that they were asking that $2,500.00 be put aside for taxes and $5,000.00 be put aside for Attorney's fees and Executor's fees, that Mr. Malloy wanted cash up front but that his Client was reluctant due to joint tenancy, that Romero wanted the household items, that the only asset is the real property, that the property is Anna Navarro's property, that the Mother wants the property distributed amongst the five Siblings, that Anna was collecting the rent and that she was the surviving joint tenant. In addition, he stated that the Family does not want a lien, that they want to pull money together and buy off Accelerated Inheritance, that Accelerated Inheritance's obligation is not a debt of the Estate, that Romero did it without Anna's permission, that the majority of the Interested Parties would like a waiver of joint tenancy or reversion of the property to the Estate of Margarita and that the Family's desire was to sell the property. Mr. Borden stated that he had no objection to having the property be in the Estate, that title may be a problem in the future, that it would be appropriate for the Court to issue an order regarding title, that the terms of the assignment transferred interest to his Client, that an accounting was needed to determine if there is any room to distribute to his Client, that cooperation of all five Parties was needed, suggested having one Fiduciary in charge to handle distribution and stated that it was in the best interest of the Estate that the property be sold. The matter was continued to June 29, 2010 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for pay off of the $17,000.00, and to see if the Family wants to sell the property through probate or change title into all 5 names.
Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-26-2010 Continued To May-17-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
